data.description:
## Context
A work ticket carries a single cryptographic attestation slot (`state["signature"]`),
written last-writer-wins by `process_signature` (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`).
Both the plan-review attestation (earned at claim time) and the completion verdict
(signed at close) land in that one slot, so closing a ticket destroys its plan-review
attestation (bug grumpy-site-beard / e6a0-ba9b-ae92-43d7). The two are independent
proofs of two different gates and must coexist, and we expect to add further
attestation kinds and to consume attestations as a per-ticket CI gate later.

## What
Replace the single slot with `state["attestations"]`, a map keyed by attestation kind
(mirroring the existing per-key-LWW `process_workflow_run`): additive across kinds,
latest-only within a kind. The kind is derived from the already-signed `manifest[0]`
(`plan-review` / `completion-verifier`), so no `PAYLOAD_VERSION` bump and every existing
signature still verifies. Reuse the `SIGNATURE` event (reducer routing only); coordinated
upgrade; new clones fall back to the legacy single `signature` for old tickets/snapshots.
Verification becomes per-kind (`verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=...)`; no kind returns a
`{kind: verdict}` map). Attestations stay immutable signed records; "valid for gate K now"
is computed on read (completion: certified AND closed AND not reopened/materially-changed
since `signed_at`; plan-review: existing freshness). Only a PASS is ever signed, so a
certified attestation implies its gate passed.

## Success Criteria
- A ticket simultaneously holds a plan-review attestation AND a completion verdict; signing
  one never clobbers the other; re-running a kind replaces only that kind.
- Round-trips through compaction/SNAPSHOT preserve all kinds; legacy single-signature
  tickets and old-clone snapshots still read and verify (fold-in fallback).
- Per-kind verification works for humans, the gates, and a future CI gate; a reopened
  ticket stops reading as completion-valid.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `state["attestations"]` map (kind-keyed, per-key LWW) replaces the single slot;
      `process_signature` routes by `data["kind"]` (fallback: parse `manifest[0]`).
- [ ] No change to the signed canonical payload; all pre-existing signatures still certify.
- [ ] Reuse `SIGNATURE` event; new clones fold a legacy single `signature` into
      `attestations` on read; compaction round-trips all kinds.
- [ ] `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=...)` certifies one kind; no kind returns
      `{kind: verdict}`; claim gate reads `plan-review`, close + child-closure read
      `completion-verifier`.
- [ ] Completion validity computed on read (certified AND closed AND not reopened/
      materially-changed since `signed_at`); attestations never mutated.
- [ ] `show`/MCP render the map (omitted when empty); docs updated; CI-readiness +
      HMAC symmetric-key constraint documented as follow-up.
- [ ] Tests: coexistence, within-kind replace, compaction round-trip, legacy read,
      reopen invalidates completion validity.
data.title: Additive, kind-keyed attestations (plan-review + completion + future coexist)
